goal: Who won the last F1 race and highlight important events
final_answer: Charles Leclerc won the 2026 British Grand Prix, with George Russell finishing second and Lewis Hamilton finishing third. Notable events during the race included Hamilton serving a five-second time penalty and Max Verstappen's car spinning out of a podium place.

# 2026 British Grand Prix. | Race 9 of 22 in the 2026 Formula One World Championship  |  |  | | --- | --- | | **←**Previous race | Next race**→** | |. The **2026 British Grand Prix** (officially known as the **Formula 1 Pirelli British Grand Prix 2026**) was a Formula One motor race that took place on 5 July 2026 at the Silverstone Circuit in Northamptonshire, England. It was the ninth round of the 2026 Formula One World Championship and the fourth of six Grands Prix in the season to utilise the sprint format. Lewis Hamilton (Ferrari) took sprint pole position ahead of Kimi Antonelli (Mercedes), with Max Verstappen (Red Bull) qualifying third. Leclerc therefore took his ninth Formula One victory, his first at the British Grand Prix and the first for a Monegasque driver in the race's history, ahead of George Russell "George Russell (racing driver)") (Mercedes), who took his first podium at Silverstone, and Hamilton.

# British Grand Prix: Ferrari's Charles Leclerc takes victory, race finishes under safety car - as it happened. Ferrari's Charles Leclerc has taken victory for the first time at the British Grand Prix. - **Ferrari's Charles Leclerc takes first victory at British Grand Prix, George Russell second**. - **British Grand Prix: Max Verstappen swears repeatedly as Red Bull car spins out of podium place**. Mercedes' George Russell crossed the line in second after the race finished under the Safety Car. Lewis Hamilton in Ferrari finished third after serving a five-second time penalty for a false start early in the race. He also had to report to the stewards' office after the race for a yellow flag infringement, but he avoided a penalty and kept his third-place podium position. Antonelli, who started in pole position, crossed the line in ninth, but was he was handed a five-second penalty for exceeding track limits. It was Leclerc's ninth race win in his Formula 1 career and he said afterwards: "It feels incredible.
